data.narrative:
Turion Space is a Series B-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $75M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of three same-stage private companies in Space & Aerospace (Cowboy Space Corporation at $2.0B, Aalyria at $1.3B, and Hubble Network at $400M), alongside a broader sector comp universe of five public names (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL).

Two valuation methods prove applicable. The stage-progression approach, anchoring to $75M total raised and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yields $142M. The peer-median method uses the three same-sector, same-stage private comparables, producing a $1.3B implied valuation with a range of $400M to $2.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of revenue disclosure. The wide dispersion between methods reflects heterogeneity within the peer set, particularly the $2.0B valuation of Cowboy Space Corporation versus the $400M floor at Hubble Network.

Triangulating the two applicable methods produces a range of $121M (low) to $1.5B (high), with a median of $721M. The substantial gap between stage-progression ($142M) and peer-median ($1.3B) points to material uncertainty around whether Turion's operational progress and market position align with high-valuation peers or mid-range comparables. Key sensitivities include near-term revenue traction, customer concentration, and the widening performance dispersion across Series B space companies.
